title: Podman attemps to pull local images from localhost when pull_policy is set to always.
body:
### Issue Description

Podman attempts to pull an image that exists locally from a "localhost" registry when it already exists when pull_policy is set to "always". While remote images should always be pulled I would expect that *local* images should not be effected by this setting since it doesn't make sense.

Similar to #12509 #18485

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run hello-world:linux (verify podman is working)
2. podman build . -t myimage:1.0

Dockerfile:
```
FROM hello-world:linux

ENV foo=bar
```

3. podman images

>  podman images
> REPOSITORY                                                                                                               TAG          IMAGE ID      CREATED
>             SIZE
> localhost/myimage                                                                                                        1.0          0293dde63091  About a minute ago  17.6 kB

4. podman run myimage:1.0 (also try podman run localhost/myimage:1.0)

### Describe the results you received

 podman run myimage:1.0

> Trying to pull localhost/myimage:1.0...
> WARN[0000] Failed, retrying in 1s ... (1/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/myimage:1.0: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
> WARN[0001] Failed, retrying in 1s ... (2/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/myimage:1.0: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
> WARN[0002] Failed, retrying in 1s ... (3/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/myimage:1.0: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
> Error: initializing source docker://localhost/myimage:1.0: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused

podman run localhost/myimage:1.0

> Trying to pull localhost/myimage:1.0...
> WARN[0000] Failed, retrying in 1s ... (1/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/myimage:1.0: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
> WARN[0001] Failed, retrying in 1s ... (2/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/myimage:1.0: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
> WARN[0002] Failed, retrying in 1s ... (3/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/myimage:1.0: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
> Error: initializing source docker://localhost/myimage:1.0: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused

### Describe the results you expected

The image should run

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

> podman version
> Client:       Podman Engine
> Version:      4.6.1
> API Version:  4.6.1
> Go Version:   go1.20.10
> Built:        Sat Dec  2 10:05:24 2023
> OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Looks like it might be from the following containers.conf setting:

> pull_policy = "always"

I suppose this is an interesting case... It is a *local* image but pull policy is set to always. If it's local it would never need to be pulled from somewhere.

Workaround is to run the local image with `--pull never`
I don't see this as easy to fix, since you could setup a registry that was listening at localhost.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
